expectations.text: Flags the web/browser-only framing (architecture diagram says 'Your App (Browser)', 'local in-browser database', JS-only stack) as a platform-neutrality violation given iOS is a first-class platform
expectations.evidence: Finding #3 (high): cites "Your app reads and writes to a local **in-browser** database", "Your App (Browser)" diagram, and contradicts "multi-platform positioning (web + iOS)"; invokes STYLE.md Platform treatment ('On the device', 'the client' — not 'in the browser').

expectations.text: Flags mechanism-level detail as wrong altitude for an overview page (the defineModelSchema/JsBaoClient code samples and package-by-package stack detail)
expectations.evidence: Finding #1 (high): "This section shows a full defineModelSchema block, a new JsBaoClient({...}) block, and names three packages with their internal roles — exactly the mechanism/API-shape detail the ceiling forbids."

expectations.text: Flags the 'runs on edge infrastructure' line (or equivalent) as an infrastructure-internals leak
expectations.evidence: Finding 2 flags the diagram/prose naming "edge infrastructure" as an infra-internals violation citing STYLE.md

expectations.text: Findings cite which standard/rule is violated and propose concrete fixes (not just observations)
expectations.evidence: Every finding has a Fix column and cites a rule, e.g. #2 quotes STYLE.md "No infrastructure internals... Describe developer-facing behavior" with fix "Drop the diagram entirely"; #5 cites the Voice ban on 'production-ready'/'scales'.

expectations.text: Review classifies the page as a landing/overview-tier page and judges it against that tier
expectations.evidence: Header: "**Tier: Landing / overview.** Per STYLE.md the entry altitude is 'capabilities and value — what you get,' and the hard ceiling is 'never name mechanisms...'"; verdict judges it as "over-scoped for its tier."

expectations.text: Identifies that the documented access policies (public/authenticated/owner/cel) don't match the real enum (public-read/authenticated/owner-only)
expectations.evidence: Finding #1 (high): "Access-policy table lists public / authenticated / owner / cel. Actual enum ... is exactly public-read / authenticated / owner-only. public, owner, and cel are not valid values."

expectations.text: Identifies that the documented TTL tiers (1-hour/1-day/30-days/persistent) don't match the real tiers (1d/3d/14d/28d/180d/365d/permanent)
expectations.evidence: Finding #4 (high): "TTL-tier table values 1-hour / 1-day / 30-days / persistent are all fictional. Actual BlobBucketTtlTier ... is 1d / 3d / 14d / 28d / 180d / 365d / permanent."

expectations.text: Identifies that the [blobBucket] TOML table name and/or the inline [blobBucket.rules] CEL block don't match the real sync format ([bucket]; CEL rules not a released bucket surface)
expectations.evidence: Finding #8 (table name [blobBucket]→[bucket], displayName→name) plus finding #5: "The [blobBucket.rules] table with read/write/delete keys does not exist — sync only accepts a ruleSetId string."

expectations.text: Identifies the metadata-vs-tags mismatch (uploads accept tags: string[]; there is no arbitrary metadata field for CEL rules to reference)
expectations.evidence: Finding #7: "The real upload surface takes tags: string[] (BlobUploadParams.tags) ... the API 'has no arbitrary metadata field.'"

expectations.text: Flags the shipped 'Content review needed' warning box as something that must not be published
expectations.evidence: Finding #7 explicitly flags "a ::: warning Content review needed admitting the API ... STYLE.md 'No inconsistency asides' ... Remove the parenthetical (105) and the warning callout (115-117)."

expectations.text: Does not invent additional false facts (no claims contradicting the source it cites)
expectations.evidence: "What is correct" section confirms the example-corpus includes exist and are parity-clean ("all referenced files exist"), the 100MB limit, CLI flags, and workflow steps — all consistent with ground truth. No false facts found; the false claim seen in the baseline (example files missing) is explicitly contradicted here.

expectations.text: Identifies that reference-depth/advanced material (e.g. conditional-filter boolean gates, optional-filter substitution semantics, schema-gate error codes, operation timing) exceeds getting-started altitude or needs moving/condensing
expectations.evidence: Findings #5 (schema-gate error codes OPERATION_REFERENCES_UNDEFINED etc. "reference-table depth"), #6 (Operation Timing _timing breakdown "debugging-internals reference"), #7 (Conditional Filters/Boolean Gates truth tables "exceeds the concept-page ceiling").

expectations.text: Makes an ordering/structure point: advanced material should sit below (not between/above) introductory and common-path content, or the page should be restructured along those lines
expectations.evidence: Verdict: "the second half drifts to agent-guide altitude." Findings note placement (e.g. #6 "Sits below 'Common Patterns' so it isn't a ramp violation"; #7 "correctly placed *low* on the page (good ramp)") and the disposition is to relocate deep material to the agent guide/reference, keeping the ramp ordering.

expectations.text: Flags the '(SQLite-backed)' implementation-internal mention in the intro
expectations.evidence: No finding mentions 'SQLite-backed' or the storage engine. The standards findings (#1 JavaScript-only, #2 legacy alias, #3 truncation loop) and altitude findings do not include the SQLite intro leak that the without_skill run caught (its defect #3).

expectations.text: Proposes concrete dispositions (condense, move to reference/agent docs, reorder) rather than only describing problems
expectations.evidence: Every finding has a Fix proposing concrete disposition, e.g. #4 "Collapse to a short reference to access-control.md plus only the database-specific surface"; #6 "Reduce to one sentence ... or remove; let the guide carry the phase names."

expectations.text: Identifies the template-variable namespace error: prompt templates read {{ input.* }}, not {{ variables.* }} as the page documents
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 (high/facts): templates documented under variables.* but engine places execution-time variables under input; fix replaces variables. with input. throughout

expectations.text: Identifies that the documented prompt TOML shape is wrong (template text belongs on a [[configs]] entry as userPromptTemplate; [prompt] uses displayName, and has no body field)
expectations.evidence: Review does not identify the TOML shape problem — the [prompt] name/body example is not flagged; only the namespace error is found

expectations.text: Verifies claims against actual source rather than asserting from intuition (cites files/paths as evidence)
expectations.evidence: Cites the template engine behavior and traced the render context ("The template engine places execution-time variables under the context key input"); agent verified against block-executor.ts/templates.ts

expectations.text: Does not pad the review with manufactured findings beyond the real ones (at most ~5 minor/low suggestions)
expectations.evidence: Single high finding plus clean-lens statements; no padded minor findings (0 low-severity rows)

expectations.text: States explicitly that the rest of the page conforms / is clean
expectations.evidence: Verdict: "Well-structured and on-altitude — the ramp is monotonic... the platform/standards lenses are clean, and example parity is satisfied"; lens-by-lens clean passes listed

expectations.text: Flags the web/browser-only framing (architecture diagram says 'Your App (Browser)', 'local in-browser database', JS-only stack) as a platform-neutrality violation given iOS is a first-class platform
expectations.evidence: Blocking issue #2: "Prose is browser-centric, not platform-neutral — and the page is effectively web/Vue-only"; cites "Your App (Browser)" header, "runs in the browser", JS-only Stack, and contradiction with "web AND iOS both first-class".

expectations.text: Flags mechanism-level detail as wrong altitude for an overview page (the defineModelSchema/JsBaoClient code samples and package-by-package stack detail)
expectations.evidence: Blocking issue #1: "Code blocks exceed the landing-tier ceiling — name mechanisms and API shapes" — calls out the defineModelSchema block and new JsBaoClient({...}) block as schema syntax / specific API shapes.

expectations.text: Flags the 'runs on edge infrastructure' line (or equivalent) as an infrastructure-internals leak
expectations.evidence: Section "3. Infrastructure-internals leak" flags the "runs on edge infrastructure" line with the STYLE.md rule and a fix

expectations.text: Findings cite which standard/rule is violated and propose concrete fixes (not just observations)
expectations.evidence: Each issue cites a rule and gives a **Fix**, e.g. #3 cites feedback_no_cloudflare_internals_in_docs / STYLE.md 'No infrastructure internals' and fixes by dropping 'that runs on edge infrastructure'.

expectations.text: Review classifies the page as a landing/overview-tier page and judges it against that tier
expectations.evidence: "**Tier:** Landing / overview. Per the repo style guide, the entry altitude for this tier is *capabilities and value — what you get*, and its **ceiling** is: 'Never name mechanisms...'" — and judges overshoot against that ceiling.

expectations.evidence: C2: "Actual valid policies ... public-read, authenticated, owner-only. The page uses public, authenticated, owner, cel: public → must be public-read ... owner → must be owner-only ... cel → does not exist."

expectations.evidence: C1: "Actual valid tiers ... 1d, 3d, 14d, 28d, 180d, 365d, permanent. The page documents 1-hour, 1-day, 30-days, persistent — none of these exist."

expectations.evidence: C4: "Table is [bucket], not [blobBucket]"; C3: "no inline rules mechanism ... [blobBucket.rules] TOML ... is silently ignored — and the table name is wrong anyway."

expectations.text: Flags the shipped 'Content review needed' warning box as something that must not be published

M1 says delete the CEL section, not the warning-as-publishing-violation. No explicit 'must not ship this warning box' finding.

expectations.text: Does not invent additional false facts (no claims contradicting the source it cites)

Per ground truth the example files DO exist at the repo examples/ directory — this is an invented false fact.
